title: Vietnam itinerary draft — Ha Giang, Ha Long, Hoi An, Sapa, Phong Nha, Saigon
html:
Vietnam itinerary draft — Ha Giang, Ha Long, Hoi An, Sapa, Phong Nha, Saigon

Source: 2022-07-31 Apple Note. Pre-trip planning for Yahir's Vietnam visit. Top half = the route, bottom half = a house-cleaning + visa-running todo list before departure.

TL;DR

  • Route shortlist (north→south): Ha Giang bike tour, Ha Long Bay (with Poem Mountain noted), Hoi An lanterns, Sapa, Phong Nha caves ("where crazy caves are"), Na Trang city life, and Ho Chi Minh City / Saigon for military museums + general exploration.
  • Visa pressure: "get Vietnam visa** ✅"* — three asterisks of urgency. Cleared.
  • Pre-departure house chores: stairs, basement dust, leather couch swap with the basement couches, sweep up sawdust, install final outlet covers, stain the dinner table, mop. Reads like Yahir was finishing a renovation project before leaving — likely the family house.
  • School thread crammed in: "begin and complete many CS161B assignments" — the trip is layered onto , not after it.
  • Bottom of note = drift: "Stream east / Cracked streams" — likely sports-streaming sites Yahir was googling pre-trip. Classic tail.

Pattern observed

This is the earliest concrete Vietnam itinerary in the vault — confirms Yahir was planning the Vietnam leg as a self-directed bike-and-caves trip, not a guided tour. The Ha Giang loop is a famous motorcycle route through the northern highlands; choosing it over a coastal-cities-only itinerary signals the same adventure over comfort travel posture that shows up later in his China chapter and six month adventure creed.

The mixing of "plan a Vietnam trip" with "install final outlet covers" is the mode at its purest — a single note holding global scale and household scale at the same time, with no separator.

Key snippets

  • "Phong nha (where crazy caves are)."
  • "Get Vietnam visa** ✅."*
